heaviest_rainfall.status: 47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No storms are expected.  The last storm was observed on 18 July 2026 and continued for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 03 July 2026, is ranked 1st among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 20 days so far.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwave right now.  There have been 3 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 28 June 2026, with only 68 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (205°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 6.9°C) and outside temperature (down 5.5°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 17%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 1941 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 2229 m), feels like temperature (up 11.3°C), dew point temperature (up 4.3°C), and outside temperature (up 8.0°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 1941.1m lower than yesterday, 2230m lower than the past week, 1464.3m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1829m to a maximum of 6260m, averaging 2280m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4510m and 3750m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures will generally be on the hot side. Outside; mild temperatures are expected. Also, pleasant humidity levels and winds will be practically absent. Showers, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and it’s dark. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; there will not be a great deal of drying overnight.
